name: Kadin Budu (Ladies' Thighs' Croquettes)
recipeInstructions:
1. Cook the mixture in about 20-25 minutes total.
2. Combine meat, boiled rice, onion, and cheese, then pass through a meat grinder using a fine screen.
3. Add herbs, salt, and pepper to taste along with 1 lightly beaten egg. Mix by hand until it forms a smooth paste.
4. Take a generous tablespoon of the mixture and form into an elongated egg shape, wider at one end than the other, or into a simpler torpedo shape.
5. Moisten your hand with water to facilitate handling.
6. Place kadin budu in the baking dish side by side as they are finished.
7. Beat remaining egg well and pour over the kadin budu, then turn them over in a dish to coat them evenly with a film of egg.
8. Place about 1/2 cup flour in a plate and roll each budu in flour one at a time, placing them into a frying pan of heated oil as they are coated.
9. Use one hand for rolling the budu in flour and keep the other hand dry for moving them to the pan.
10. Fry over high heat until golden brown, turning frequently with tongs so that they maintain their shape.
11. Drain on paper towels and serve hot with vegetable or salad accompaniment.
